response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of an open-source geo-data platform, web GIS applications) and has built low-latency, high-throughput systems in DLang using async I/O, fibers, and caching — skills that conceptually transfer to routing pipeline work. However, their modern C++ exposure is dated (an early-career role on NI LabWindows/CVI), they list no Rust, and their recent decade has been predominantly TypeScript/Node full-stack and web product work. They have no direct experience with routing engines, map tiling, or navigation guidance systems, which are core to this role.
strengths: Founded and maintains an open-source geospatial data platform used in production
Low-latency systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, caching) in DLang
Strong Linux, Docker, CI/CD, and testing/craftsmanship background
concerns: C++ experience is old and limited; no Rust listed despite it being the core requirement
No routing, map tiling, or navigation guidance system experience
Recent roles are web/full-stack product work, distant from data pipeline engineering
